description: A V8 sandbox bypass vulnerability exists in Runtime_TierUpWasmToJSWrapper (src/runtime/runtime-wasm.cc). When a JS function is exported from one Wasm module and imported into another module's indirect function table, WasmImportData::call_origin is set to an in-sandbox (untrusted) Tuple2 object containing (WasmInstanceObject, Smi call_origin_index). The sign of the Smi encodes the origin type: negative means import table index, positive means indirect function table index. In Runtime_TierUpWasmToJSWrapper, the runtime reads call_origin_index directly from the untrusted Tuple2 without verifying it is consistent with the actual signature stored in the trusted WasmImportData. An attacker can corrupt the in-sandbox Tuple2 to set call_origin_index to a negative Smi, causing CallOriginIsImportIndex() to return true. This forces the runtime to look up sig_index from a different function's canonical signature (from call_origin_module->functions[func_index]) rather than validating it against import_data->sig(). The existing SBXCHECK(sig_index.valid()) only confirms a signature was found, but cannot detect the mismatch between the retrieved sig_index and the actual sig from the trusted WasmImportData. The compiled WasmToJsWrapper is then compiled using sig (correct) but installed under the wrong sig_index (confused), creating a signature confusion that results in calling a JS function with an incorrect calling convention and enabling arbitrary address read/write outside the V8 sandbox.

fixes.description: Minimal compatibility fix: derive Runtime_TierUpWasmToJSWrapper's canonical signature index from trusted WasmImportData::sig() and validate table call_origin only as an origin check.
fixes.evidence_text: 0d15bbf1fb92f435e10c14f858d82d4cca851bf4 [wasm] Store canonicalized signature pointers on imports

fixes.description: Reject corrupted cross-instance WasmImportData call origins before wrapper tier-up when the Tuple2 origin no longer encodes a table slot.
